markdown:
#  About the Procore Standard Budget View 
##  Background 
All Procore company accounts include the 'Procore Standard Budget' view, which is the default view that is available for use with the project's Budget tool. This view can also be customized as described in [Set up a New Budget View](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/admin-company/tutorials/set-up-a-new-budget-view).
##### Example
**Procore Standard Budget View**
The illustration below shows you the default layout for the 'Procore Standard Budget' view, as it looks when assigned to a project's Budget tool.
![Budget Standard Budget View](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/1MhOBLUsNDU617SUBPTd92/a09ba5b09d975297f45b196e325a9707/budget-standard-budget-view.png?fm=webp)
## See Also
  * [What are Procore's standard budget views?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-are-procores-standard-budget-views)
  * [Which budget views should I add to my Procore projects?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-which-budget-views-should-i-add-to-my-projects)
  * [What are the default statuses for change orders in Procore?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-are-the-default-statuses-for-change-orders-in-procore)
title: About the Procore Standard Budget View
description: This tutorial provides you with basic information about the Procore Standard Budget View.
